chunk_summary: This section outlines outpatient diagnostic strategies and oral iron response monitoring for pediatric anemia under India's Anemia Mukt Bharat program, emphasizing practical phenotype assessment, therapeutic trials, and adherence verification.
learning_objectives: Understand the diagnostic approach for pediatric iron deficiency anemia in outpatient settings.
Learn therapeutic oral iron dosing guidelines and response monitoring protocols.
Identify causes of nonresponse to oral iron therapy and appropriate follow-up actions.
key_facts: Iron deficiency anemia is suggested by pallor, microcytosis, high RDW, and poor dietary iron intake.
Therapeutic oral iron dose: 3–6 mg/kg/day of elemental iron; lower doses (2–3 mg/kg/day) may improve adherence due to fewer gastrointestinal side effects.
A hemoglobin rise of ≥1 g/dL after 2 weeks supports iron responsiveness.
Treatment duration: 8–12 weeks to normalize hemoglobin and replenish iron stores.
Ferritin levels should be interpreted alongside CRP due to inflammation-related elevation.
reference_quote: The therapeutic oral dose is usually 3–6 mg/kg/day of elemental iron (not '5 mL syrup'). Many children respond to 2–3 mg/kg/day with fewer gastrointestinal adverse effects, improving adherence.
document_key_topics: Outpatient diagnostic approach for pediatric anemia
Oral iron therapy dosing and response monitoring
